name: Chicken Etoufee
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup Onion, half finely cut and half roughly cut
recipeInstructions:
1. Pre-cook the chicken breasts in the microwave for about 10 minutes on high. Set aside to cool.
2. When cool, pull into strips about the size of a small finger (or use an equal amount of leftover roast chicken).
3. In a cast iron skillet over medium-high heat, singe the rough-cut onions until lightly browned (about 3-5 minutes).
4. Add the remaining vegetables, garlic, and spices to the skillet. Stir in the butter and cook over medium heat until the vegetables are limp (about 5 minutes).
5. Stir in the dry roux, then add the chicken broth and simmer uncovered over low heat for 15 minutes.
6. Add the cooked chicken and continue to simmer for another 10-15 minutes, or until the sauce is thickened to a thin gravy consistency.
7. Serve over rice. Makes two generous servings.
SHRIMP ETOUFE
Follow the same recipe as Chicken Etoufee, but:
- Use one small package of shrimp (5-6 oz.)
- Decrease vegetable amounts to 1/2 cup each
- Reduce spice amount to 1 teaspoon Cajun chicken mix
- Decrease roux to 2 tablespoons
- Decrease butter to 2 pats
- Decrease broth to 1 cup
- Do not precook the shrimp
- Add uncooked shrimp at the end and simmer for only 2-3 minutes. Top with a dash of Cajun Sunshine if desired.
